conditions: Diabetic peripheral neuropathy (mild, intermittent tingling in feet)
health.mental_health: Since the diagnosis last year, Taylor has periods of health anxiety and low mood, especially around lab results and medication side effects; they use a journaling app and are considering short-term therapy but are cautious about confidentiality.
recent_visits: Endocrinology follow-up at Lagos University Teaching Hospital (LUTH) for HbA1c review and thyroid dose adjustment (6 weeks ago)
Cardiology clinic visit for blood pressure optimization and ambulatory BP monitoring (3 months ago)
Eye screening at a private ophthalmology clinic in Ikeja for diabetic retinopathy check (2 months ago)
Dietitian consult at a private clinic in Victoria Island for carb counting and weight management plan (last month)
Lab work (HbA1c, lipid panel, TSH/T4, kidney function) at a diagnostics center in Yaba (2 weeks ago)

finance.income_range: ₦650,000–₦1,100,000 per month (net, depending on on-call allowances)
finance.debt: Ongoing repayment of a small personal loan used to cover initial specialist consultations, labs, and a home blood pressure monitor; also carries a revolving credit balance from pharmacy and supermarket expenses during medication changes.
investments: Small monthly savings in USD via a domiciliary account for emergencies
recent_transactions: Pharmacy purchase: metformin, levothyroxine, amlodipine, atorvastatin (monthly refill)
Diagnostics center payment for HbA1c, lipid panel, thyroid function tests
Purchase of a glucometer, test strips, and lancets
Subscription renewal for cloud backup and password manager (personal)

relationships: Partner: Ifeoma Okorie (works in fashion retail; helps with meal planning and reminders, but they sometimes argue about Taylor's strict diet)
Older sister: Zinhle Nkomo (lives in Abuja; checks in often and pushes Taylor to disclose more to extended family)
Close friend: Tunde Adebayo (gym buddy who knows about the diabetes but not the thyroid condition)
A small WhatsApp support group for Nigerians managing Type 2 diabetes (mostly anonymous handles)
social.social_media_activity: Keeps a low profile online; uses Twitter/X and LinkedIn for tech content and career networking, avoids posting health updates, and has tightened privacy settings after receiving unsolicited 'herbal cure' DMs.
private_conversations: Discussing whether to tell their manager about the chronic illness to reduce overnight on-call duties
Concerns about sexual side effects and fatigue affecting intimacy
Worries about long-term complications (kidney health, vision) and fear of being judged as 'unhealthy'
Navigating being non-binary with some family members while dealing with medical appointments that misgender them

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri 9:00–18:00 with rotating on-call (1 week per month) and occasional late-night incident response
regular_appointments: Monthly endocrinology follow-up (or every 6–8 weeks when adjusting medication)
Weekly home blood pressure checks logged for doctor review
Biweekly evening walk/low-impact workout session with a friend (weather permitting)
upcoming_events: Next lab draw (HbA1c, TSH/T4, creatinine) scheduled for next Tuesday morning in Yaba
Endocrinologist appointment in two weeks at LUTH
Work change-freeze weekend for a core network upgrade (expected Saturday night)
Family visit to Abuja planned for a long weekend next month (still tentative depending on health)
schedule.daily_routine: Wakes around 6:30, checks fasting glucose, takes levothyroxine, then prepares a low-sugar breakfast; commutes to Ikeja/Yaba corridor depending on site needs; takes blood pressure meds mid-morning; tries to walk 20–30 minutes after dinner, logs readings and meals in a tracking app, and aims for sleep by 23:00 unless on-call.

professional.company: Mid-sized fintech payment processing company based in Victoria Island with a small data center footprint and hybrid cloud infrastructure
projects: Migrating legacy Windows Server workloads to Azure and hardening identity policies (MFA/Conditional Access)
Implementing centralized logging/monitoring (SIEM) for compliance and incident response
Quarterly patch management cycle and vulnerability remediation across endpoints and servers
Network segmentation and firewall rule cleanup ahead of an external audit
professional.performance_issues: Taylor has had a few mornings of late arrival after poor sleep during medication adjustments and one on-call incident where they needed a colleague to take over due to dizziness; they are trying to formalize a backup rota without disclosing full medical details.
workplace_relationships: Direct manager (Chinedu Eze) is supportive about flexible hours but expects reliable on-call coverage
A senior developer frequently escalates urgent requests, which increases Taylor's stress during health flare-ups
Close colleague (Amina Bello) informally swaps on-call shifts when Taylor has appointments, but Taylor worries about being seen as unreliable

hobbies: Home cooking and experimenting with lower-carb Nigerian meals (e.g., smaller portions of swallow, more vegetables)
Weekend photography walks around Lekki Conservation Centre and nearby neighborhoods
Reading sci-fi and African speculative fiction
habits: Tracks blood glucose and blood pressure readings daily in a mobile app
Meal-preps on Sundays to avoid high-carb takeout during busy workweeks
Limits alcohol to rare occasions after noticing BP spikes
Occasional late-night troubleshooting during on-call weeks that disrupts sleep
Keeps emergency snacks and glucose tablets in their work bag
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a one-bedroom apartment in Ikeja and lives with their partner; they keep a small drawer of medical supplies (glucometer strips, meds) out of sight when visitors come over to avoid questions.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Reduced sugar and refined carbs; prioritizes vegetables, beans, fish, and lean proteins; watches salt intake for hypertension; tries to keep consistent meal timing to avoid glucose swings, but struggles when work incidents run late.
